stories.headline: Stock Market Today March 30, 2026: Sensex tanks 1,191 points, Nifty50 Fall To 22,470 Indian equities on Dalal Street saw volatility as global market trends and fresh tariff concerns linked to Donald Trump impacted investor sentiment.
stories.summary: Indian equities plummeted on March 30, 2026, with the Sensex tanking 1,191 points and the Nifty50 falling to 22,470. The decline was attributed to global market trends and fresh tariff concerns linked to Donald Trump, impacting investor sentiment. The Sensex closed at 71,947.55, down 2.22%, while the Nifty ended at 22,331.40, down 2.14%. The sell-off was broad-based, with realty and financials dragging the market down. The India VIX rose 6%, indicating increased volatility. Market experts remain cautious on the near-term outlook, highlighting the 22,500-22,470 zone as a crucial level to watch. Despite a surge in the US market, with the Dow jumping 600 points, Indian equities failed to gain momentum, ending the day sharply lower. The Nifty50 opened below 22,500, with the Sensex down over 1,100 points at one point.

stories.headline: Stock market today (March 30, 2026): Which are the top gainers and losers in Nifty50 and BSE Sensex today? Check list India Business News: Equity benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty ended the last trading session of the 2025-26 financial year sharply lower on Monday, as the ongo
stories.summary: Equity benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty ended the last trading session of the 2025-26 financial year sharply lower on Monday. The 30-share BSE Sensex dropped 1,690.23 points, or 2.25%, to close at 73,583.22. The decline was attributed to global weakness and rising geopolitical tensions, which dented investor sentiment. The stock market will reopen on March 25, 2026. Meanwhile, global markets saw a surge, with the Dow jumping 600 points and the S&P 500 and Nasdaq also rising, as Trump signaled talks with Iran, causing oil prices to tumble. The top gainers and losers in Nifty50 and BSE Sensex will be closely watched when the market reopens. Investors can track live updates, share prices, and market news through platforms like Angel One, which offers share market live updates and IPO details.

stories.headline: Stock Market Crash News Today Updates: Nifty Below 22.500, Sensex Falls Nearly 1,200 Points; HDFC Bank, Axis Bank Top Losers Thirteen out of 15 sectoral indices compiled by the NSE declined, led by the NSE Nifty Bank Index's 2.1%.
stories.summary: The Indian stock market experienced a significant decline, with the Nifty falling below 22,500 and the Sensex dropping nearly 1,200 points. Thirteen out of 15 sectoral indices compiled by the NSE declined, led by the NSE Nifty Bank Index's 2.1% fall. Major losers included HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Bajaj Finance, and State Bank of India. The decline occurred amid weekly and monthly F&O expiry, with all 15 sectoral gauges compiled by the NSE declining. The Nifty Bank and PSU Bank indices were down over 2%, with Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, and SBI being major laggards. The market slump was notable, especially during the last 30-minute trade, with the Sensex and Nifty settling lower.

stories.headline: Share Market Live Updates March 30: Stock to buy today: Karur Vysya Bank (₹295.85) Stock Market on 30 March 2026 | Share Market Updates Find here all the updates related to Sensex, Nifty, BSE, NSE, share prices and Indian stock markets.
stories.summary: Karur Vysya Bank's stock is recommended for purchase at ₹295.85, with a potential rise to ₹375 over the medium-term. However, investors should be cautious of a possible dip to ₹280. The stock recently formed a bullish engulfing pattern following last week's rally. On March 30, 2026, the Indian markets closed sharply lower, with the Sensex and Nifty tumbling over 2% as FY26 came to a close. Karur Vysya Bank's Q4 results were previously announced, with the bank's performance details available. The current stock price and potential future movements indicate a buying opportunity, but investors should monitor the stock's movement and be prepared for potential fluctuations.

stories.headline: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang says ‘we’ve achieved AGI.’ But no one can agree on what that means Why the most important term in tech remains hotly debated.
stories.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang recently stated that artificial general intelligence (AGI) has already been achieved. However, the statement sparked debate as there is no consensus on what AGI means. Huang's claim was made during a podcast interview with Lex Fridman, where he discussed the advancements in AI systems designing and improving themselves. Recent research contradicts Huang's statement, suggesting that AGI has not been achieved and proposing new methods to measure it. The term AGI remains hotly debated, with different interpretations of what it entails, including the ability of AI to create and improve upon its own work, making significant leaps in technology. The lack of a clear definition has led to varying opinions on whether AGI has been achieved, highlighting the need for a standardized understanding of the term.

stories.headline: Google Unveils New AI Model Capable of Learning from Limited Data
stories.summary: Google has unveiled its new AI model, Gemini, a family of multimodal large language models developed by Google DeepMind. Gemini is the successor to LaMDA and PaLM 2, and it includes various versions such as Gemini Pro, Gemini Deep Think, Gemini Flash, and Gemini Flash Lite. The model was announced on December 6, 2023, and its latest version, Gemini 3.1 Pro, boasts incredible reasoning powers, enabling simultaneous, multi-step tasks. Gemini 3.1's agentic capabilities can build more helpful and intelligent personal AI assistants. Additionally, Google has released Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a real-time multimodal voice model for low-latency audio, video, and tool use for AI agents. The company has also introduced Google AI Ultra, which includes everything in Google AI Pro, plus more, and is available for users over 18.

stories.headline: Meta Releases Groundbreaking AI-Powered Chatbot with Human-Like Conversations
stories.summary: Meta has released a groundbreaking AI-powered chatbot capable of human-like conversations, built with Llama 4. The new Meta AI app is a step toward creating a more personal AI experience. This launch follows the success of other AI chatbot platforms, such as Moltbook, which gained millions of registered bots within days. Meta's AI chatbots can remember previous conversations, send personalized follow-up messages, and re-engage users without prompts. The technology has the potential to significantly impact social media, with some experts suggesting that chatbots may soon produce a substantial portion of online content. The new app is part of Meta's broader efforts to integrate AI into its platforms, including WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, and Messenger, where people already interact with Meta AI daily. The development marks a significant advancement in AI-powered conversation technology.

stories.headline: Microsoft Invests Heavily in AI Research to Develop More Advanced Machine Learning Algorithms
stories.summary: Microsoft is heavily investing in AI research to develop advanced machine learning algorithms. The company has allocated $80 billion for AI infrastructure, a $25 billion increase from the previous year, after laying off 15,000 employees in 2025. Research has shown that offloading robotics AI workloads to edge GPUs can improve response times dramatically. Microsoft's investment in OpenAI, estimated at $13 billion, grants access to large language models like ChatGPT. The company aims to democratize machine learning, making AI and ML technologies accessible to all. New algorithms for adaptive optimization and coordination have been developed, along with benchmarks and evaluation protocols to capture group-level objectives. These investments and developments are part of Microsoft's efforts to advance its AI capabilities and integrate them into its products and services.
